algae_description: Brown/diatom algae coating substrate and sponge filter surfaces; some green filamentous algae visible on plant stems and lower hardscape areas
substrate_condition: Dark substrate with visible organic debris, plant matter, and light biofilm accumulation — characteristic of early-cycle establilshment
concerns: Slight cloudiness typical of day-25 cycling — biofilm and bacterial bloom expected
Brown diatom algae heavy on surfaces — normal for maturing biofilm, nothing alarming
Substrate shows decent detritus base forming — good sign for bacterial colonization
narrative: Day 25 settling in nicely. The tank is doing exactly what it should be at this stage — building biofilm, hosting diatom blooms, and establishing the bacterial foundation. Three shrimp are visibly grazing; the rest are where they should be, tucked into cover. Water's slightly cloudy but that's the cycle speaking, not a warning. Keep watching, keep testing — we're right on track.
owner_comment: shrimp hanging out by the sponges and 2 in the christmas moss. grazing quietly and pretty calm. did see more molts this morning and think they prefer stiller water.

algae_description: Bright green spot algae (likely green dust or similar microalgae) scattered across the substrate surface and lower tank regions; distributed sparsely in small patches throughout visible area. Healthy sign during cycling.
substrate_condition: Dark, uniform grain appearance — clean overall with minimal visible debris. Substrate supports plants well. Some fine organic matter settling visible but within normal limits for a cycling tank.
narrative: Day 25 of the cycle looks quiet and steady. The colony is establishing — a handful of shrimp visible, most likely sheltering in the hardscape (not visible from this angle). The green plant cluster is holding strong, and that spotty green algae blooming across the substrate is exactly what you want to see at this stage. Water clarity is excellent. The cycling is doing its job, and the tank is building its foundation well.

algae_description: Bright green filamentous algae or plant growth clustered on the left side of the substrate, appearing healthy and vibrant. Some yellowish biofilm visible on tank glass surfaces, typical of early cycle biofilm colonization.
substrate_condition: Fine gravel, evenly distributed and clean. Substrate shows good texture with no visible detritus pockets. Well-maintained appearance.
concerns: Slight cloudiness consistent with nitrogen cycle phase
Glass shows biofilm/algae film which is expected during cycle establishment
narrative: Day 25 into the nitrogen cycle, and the tank is settling in nicely. The water has that characteristic slight haze you get mid-cycle—nothing alarming, just the bacterial colonies doing their work. The green growth on the left is thriving, and the substrate is clean and stable. A couple shrimp visible; the rest are likely tucked into hiding spots, which is typical cycling behavior. Equipment running smoothly."
owner_comment: tank view. help me sex some of these shrimp with the closeups?

algae_description: Bright green filamentous algae growth visible on the left side of the frame, attached to hardscape or substrate. Fine, wispy appearance typical of early-stage growth.
substrate_condition: Small rounded pebble/gravel substrate in good condition. Evenly distributed, no visible accumulation of detritus or debris. Well-maintained appearance.
visible_algae_details: Green filamentous algae appearing on left side near substrate level
concerns: Early-stage green filamentous algae growth — monitor for spreading but not alarming at this cycle phase
Day 25 of nitrogen cycle: ammonia and nitrite should be declining; ammonia conversion underway. Confirm with manual test if not logged recently.
narrative: One vibrant red cherry shrimp holding position on the substrate — she looks alert and well-colored. The tank is crystalline and the colony's grazing grounds are pristine. I'm seeing the first hint of green algae on the left hardscape, which is expected at this stage as the biofilter matures and nutrient cycling begins. Nothing to worry about yet, but worth watching as we move deeper into the cycle.

algae_description: Fine green filamentous algae growing on plant stems and foliage; minimal substrate coverage; early bloom stage
substrate_condition: Clean, well-established pebble substrate with no visible detritus or debris accumulation
concerns: Early green algae bloom on plant mass (expected during nitrogen cycle)
Some plant stems showing slight browning/damage (natural acclimation or minor algae pressure)
narrative: Day 25 of cycle — the tank is settling in nicely. One red cherry visible near the plants, foraging. Water is crystal clear, and the planted area is lush and green. Fine filamentous algae is beginning to colonize the plant stems, which is par for the course this early in the nitrogen cycle. The substrate looks pristine and well-sorted. Growth is happening.

algae_description: Green spot algae and thin film algae visible on substrate and hardscape surfaces; yellow-green algal growth on right side plant stems and glass
substrate_condition: Dark sand/soil substrate with scattered debris and algal film coating; porous texture suggests active biofilm colonization — normal for day 25 of cycling
concerns: Slight water cloudiness consistent with biofilm development during cycle establishment
Moderate algae growth on hardscape and plants — typical for nitrogen cycle phase but warrants monitoring to prevent overgrowth
narrative: Day 25: one red cherry shrimp visible, foraging calmly across the substrate. Water shows expected slight turbidity — the tank's microbiome is building. Algae is present and spreading on the hardscape and plant foliage, which is textbook for this stage of the cycle. The substrate is visibly colonized with biofilm. This is the work phase of establishment, not a warning sign.
